text: Delivered Eigen.eigSymmetricGeneralized (dense symmetric-definite path per parity §4): Cholesky-of-B reduction, inverse-free C = L^-1 A L^-T via two per-column triangular solve stages using the C-symmetry trick (L C = Yt — valid because A is symmetrized first; reviewer verified the identity breaks without aSym and confirmed the code uses aSym), symmetrize before kernel, back-transform via strided upper solve on L.t. B-orthonormal vectors, true generalized residuals against original A/B (never against C — near-singular SPD B stays honest), orthogonalityError = |XtBX - I|_F. Selection legality reused from the symmetric dense path. Scope: operator/iterative generalized Lanczos OUT (noted for follow-up), QZ left wholly to spectral-backend-boundary (no stub). Adversarial review: no bugs; polish applied by lead (kappa(B) sensitivity scaladoc, parity doc row updated to shipped name eigSymmetricGeneralized). 8 tests incl. analytic diagonal pencil, random pencil vs naive-reduction cross-check, lower-triangle-only bit-identity, NotPositiveDefinite, full Left matrix. Verified: coreJVM 275/275, coreJS 272/272, laws 17/17 both.
